heading: Public access to State disbursement records
label: 1306a Public access to State disbursement records
text: No State or any agency or political subdivision thereof shall be deprived of any grant-in-aid or other payment to which it otherwise is or has become entitled pursuant to subchapter I (other than section 303(a)(3) thereof), IV, X, XIV, or XVI (other than section 1383(a)(3) thereof) of this chapter, by reason of the enactment or enforcement by such State of any legislation prescribing any conditions under which public access may be had to records of the disbursement of any such funds or payments within such State, if such legislation prohibits the use of any list or names obtained through such access to such records for commercial or political purposes.
notes: References in TextSection 303(a)(3), referred to in text, was repealed by , , .Pub. That section was amended generally by , , . However, the amendment by was inapplicable to Puerto Rico, Guam, and the Virgin Islands, so that the prior section (which is set out as a note under ) continues in effect for Puerto Rico, Guam, and the Virgin Islands.July 25, 1962Oct. 30, 1972Pub. L. 87–543, title I, § 141(a)76 Stat. 200Pub. L. 92–603, § 30186 Stat. 1478Pub. L. 92–603section 1383 of this title
CodificationSection was enacted as part of act , popularly known as the Revenue Act of 1951, and not as part of the Social Security Act which comprises this chapter.Oct. 20, 1951
